review_notes: The functions provided are applicable to Kubernetes and map well to the NIST AC-24(2) control by focusing on access control decisions without relying on user or process identity. The function names are mostly clear and follow the naming conventions. However, the function 'k8s_rbac_service_account_token_automount_disabled' could be more descriptive to indicate it prevents unauthorized access by disabling automatic token mounting.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_rbac_service_account_token_automount_disabled' to 'k8s_rbac_service_account_token_automount_prevented' for clarity on the intent of preventing unauthorized access.
title: Access Control Decisions - Access control decisions | no user or process identity enforce access control de
step1_reasoning: The control AC-24(2) focuses on enforcing access control decisions without relying on user or process identity. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that roles and permissions are appropriately set to prevent overly permissive access. This involves ensuring no cluster-wide administrative privileges are granted unnecessarily, enforcing least privilege principles, and managing service account tokens to prevent unauthorized access.
